comments.author: F2Scout
comments.text: Korv Batt finally delivers on the promise. Three purple sectors in SQ3, pole by 0.017 seconds, then a sprint win where he lost the lead to Porter and calmly took it back on lap 15. That's not just speed — that's racecraft. The Singaporean has been the quiet one in the Prema garage while Price grabbed headlines with recovery drives. Now he's third in the championship and Prema are P3 in the constructors'. Al-Rashid's 'mark my words: he is a contender' feels like the moment the paddock woke up to what Mercedes saw at that junior shootout. Melon failing to score in SQ2 and Dubišar out in SQ1 means the championship has been blown wide open. Porter leads, but the top five are separated by 9 points.

replies.author: AnonymousGrunt_Fan
replies.text: Grunt finished 19th in the sprint. 19th. From a driver who won the feature in Melbourne. His qualifying pace has completely vanished since Australia. He's now fifth in the standings, 9 points behind Porter. The feature race tomorrow is massive — if he can't qualify in the top ten, the title challenge is on life support.

replies.author: BattSignal
replies.text: I've been waiting for this since his debut season! Batt finished eighth in 2025 with glimpses of pace but never quite put it together. Now he's a race winner. The move to retake Porter on lap 15 was textbook — brave but measured. Prema have two drivers in the top ten now: Batt P3 on 28 points, Price in the midfield. The Mercedes junior programme is delivering.

comments.author: KodiakCallahanFan
comments.text: Callahan and Asdelian duelling for third was the best on-track battle of the sprint. DAMS came away with both cars in the top four, and suddenly they're P2 in the constructors'. Callahan's consistency is underrated — he's been in the points at every round. Asdelian's aggression finally paid off after that disastrous ultra-long stint in Bahrain. DAMS are quietly becoming the most complete team on the grid.

replies.author: AsdelianApologist
replies.text: Asdelian's three attempts before finally passing Callahan was the kind of patience he didn't have in 2025. Last year he'd have lunged and taken both cars out. This year he waited, set it up, and executed. The Alpine Academy have to be pleased with his development. If he can keep this up, he's a dark horse for the title.

comments.author: MittensMania
comments.text: Mittens locked up on lap 1 and fell to last. Finished 14th. After the Bahrain disaster — lap-1 spin, unforced second spin, P17 — this is becoming a pattern. The consistency that defined his Melbourne weekend has evaporated. Hitech need to give him a car that doesn't bite on cold tyres, or this championship is slipping away. He's only 17 points off the lead, but the trend is alarming.

replies.author: CatmanCarlo
replies.text: Mittens' superpower was always that he scored even on bad days. Now he's not scoring at all. The lock-up was uncharacteristic — he's usually so clean. Something's off with the Hitech setup on low-grip surfaces. They need to fix it for the feature.

comments.author: PremierFan
comments.text: Premier's mechanical DNF while running in the top ten is devastating. After that pole in Bahrain and the podium in the feature, he was building real momentum. Now he's sitting in the cockpit, race suit unzipped, watching his championship hopes fade. VAR had both cars in the points before the retirement — Frid finished P6 — but the team's luck this season has been brutal. Premier is too talented to stay down. The feature race is a chance to bounce back.

replies.author: FridTracker
replies.text: Frid P6 keeps VAR in the constructors' fight. He's not flashy, but he's consistently in the points. The VCARB Academy driver is doing exactly what's expected: solid, reliable, building a foundation. If Premier can get a clean feature race, VAR could leave Jeddah with a strong points haul despite the DNF.

comments.author: ChaosReigns
comments.text: F2 Jeddah sprint summary: Batt wins his first race from pole, Porter takes the championship lead, Melon fails to score in SQ2, Dubišar out in SQ1, Premier suffers a mechanical DNF, Callahan and Asdelian duel for third, Mittens locks up and struggles again, a guest driver named Ricardo Kaka finishes P7 on debut, and the top five in the championship are separated by 9 points. Three different winners in three rounds. The feature race grid will be set by a separate qualifying session. This championship is absolutely wide open.

replies.author: VoiceOfTheFan
replies.text: BATT WON! PORTER LEADS THE CHAMPIONSHIP! MELON OUT IN SQ2! THIS F2 SEASON IS WILD!

comments.author: CamposDespair
comments.text: Olmedo P10, Aizawa nowhere. At least Olmedo scored. Campos are still last in the constructors', but the margins are tiny. Aizawa's pace has disappeared since Melbourne. The Ferrari Academy junior needs a result in the feature or the pressure is going to mount.

comments.author: McLarenJuniorWatch
comments.text: Melon out in SQ2, finishing 15th in the championship leader's worst session of the season. The Rodin car looked unsettled in the high-speed corners, and Hongnoi didn't feature either. McLaren's F2 juniors had a rough day. But Batt's win — he's a Mercedes junior — shows the depth of talent across the academies. The feature race qualifying is a fresh start.

comments.author: MelonMomentum
comments.text: Melon's first real stumble of the season. SQ2 elimination, no points. He's now second in the championship, three behind Porter. The feature race grid will be set by a separate qualifying session, so he can reset. But the Rodin car needs to find pace in Jeddah's high-speed sections, or the lead Porter built today could stretch further tomorrow.

comments.author: DubišarDoubter
comments.text: Dubišar out in SQ1. The Bahrain sprint winner, eliminated at the first hurdle. 1:31.760 was nowhere near good enough. He's now fourth in the championship after leading it briefly. The Invicta car clearly has pace — Porter is leading the championship — so this is a driver issue. Dubišar's one-lap pace has been inconsistent all season. He needs to fix it before the feature.

comments.author: StatsNerd_26
comments.text: Quick championship update after the Jeddah sprint: Porter 34, Melon 31, Batt 28, Dubišar 26, Grunt 25. Top five separated by 9 points. Three different winners from three rounds: Grunt (Melbourne feature), Dubišar (Bahrain sprint), Batt (Jeddah sprint). Constructors: Invicta 60, DAMS 45, Prema 38, AIX 33. The feature race could completely reshuffle this again.
